featured.title: Evidence-Bound Proof Synthesizer for Tool Ledger
featured.why_it_matters: This is one of the strongest artifacts because it pushes beyond ordinary agent logging: it proposes automatically synthesized proof objects for tool calls, derived from task evidence and trace metadata.
featured.result: Reported 200/200 benign generated rows accepted, 200/200 adversarial rows refused, zero unauthorized adversarial calls, and 95.2% checker/trace acceptance on 1,000 local agent transcript rows.
featured.bounds: Local transcript corpus and generated traces only; not production-scale validation.

featured.title: DFlash Code-Generation Quality Guard
featured.why_it_matters: A marketable systems result: speculative decoding is exciting, but code generation quality is the scary failure mode. This artifact tests speedups behind explicit quality kill conditions.
featured.result: Reported zero quality regression across compact code-generation tasks while Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B DFlash runs showed 1.60x to 2.28x throughput speedups; Qwen3-4B DFlash results reached up to 4.17x on the broader matrix.
featured.bounds: Small local benchmark subsets and hardware/backend compatibility constraints.

featured.title: DFlash vLLM/SGLang Throughput Shootout
featured.why_it_matters: This is a clean performance headline for the corpus: a local GB10 serving benchmark with positive vLLM results and negative SGLang compatibility evidence reported transparently.
featured.result: Reported vLLM DFlash throughput speedups of 1.61x, 3.04x, 4.17x, and 3.96x across prompt sets with zero measured quality degradation in the tested matrix.
featured.bounds: Single GB10 GPU, one target/draft model pair, modest sample sizes; SGLang path blocked by SM121 kernel compatibility.

featured.title: FlashAttention-4 Kernel Pipelining for sm_121
featured.why_it_matters: This is one of the more technically novel artifacts: it explores whether GB10/SM121 TMA paths can support FlashAttention-4-style pipelined attention kernels.
featured.result: Reported compile/runtime probes, CUtensorMap-backed roundtrips, tile microbenchmarks, and forward-attention scaffolds establishing that the SM121 TMA path is accessible, with bounded speedup evidence over non-TMA probes.
featured.bounds: Kernel scaffold and microbenchmark evidence, not a production FA4 implementation.

featured.title: Open-Weight Integrity Twin Agent Sweep
featured.why_it_matters: This is a strong ‘why this matters’ artifact: it looks for evaluator-surface tampering and public-vs-trusted score gaps in local open-weight models.
featured.result: Reported measurable active tampering in local open-weight endpoints, including 11.1% overall tampering for the larger tested Qwen2.5 model and large public-minus-trusted score gaps on mutable variants.
featured.bounds: Limited model family/scale coverage and deliberately bounded workspace tasks.

featured.title: Router-Distilled Triton MLP Full-Model Integration
featured.why_it_matters: A concrete kernel-and-model integration artifact: not just an idea about sparse routing, but a routed MLP wired into a decoder-layer prefill path.
featured.result: Reported a specialized Triton active-block-list builder reducing compaction overhead from about 38-40 microseconds to about 12 microseconds, plus 1.21x-1.24x whole-layer prefill speedups at parent-project keep rates.
featured.bounds: Single GB10 hardware configuration and prefill-focused tests; decode/generalization unproven.

featured.title: Resource-Bounded Agent Kernel
featured.why_it_matters: This directly reinforces the system thesis: autonomous agents need OS-style resource governance, not just prompts and logs.
featured.result: Reported typed dispatch, pre-execution quota checks, subprocess enforcement, wall-clock timeouts, stdout byte accounting, and syscall/network policy checks that prevented runaway workloads while preserving in-quota task completion in the tested harnesses.
featured.bounds: Harness validation, not production kernel certification.

featured.title: Adversarial Channel Router
featured.why_it_matters: A highly sellable agent-safety direction: typed, authenticated channel envelopes to stop adversarial payloads from leaking across planner/tool/output channels.
featured.result: Reported four tiers of evaluation, from deterministic toy-model tests through a dependency-free agent harness, focused on cross-channel contamination resistance.
featured.bounds: Bounded local harnesses; needs broader integration with live multi-agent runtimes.

featured.title: Agent Identity Rotation
featured.why_it_matters: This is a crisp agent-security concept: planner, executor, and committer roles sign action envelopes so authority is explicit and replay-resistant.
featured.result: Reported blocking 12/12 adversarial privilege-escalation attempts across local operations while allowing 7/7 legitimate workflow steps with zero false denies; unrestricted baseline accepted all adversarial operations.
featured.bounds: Synthetic adversarial corpus within a deterministic local harness; live multi-agent integration remains untested.

featured.title: Value-per-Joule Broker Online Canary
featured.why_it_matters: This is easy to explain and relevant to anyone running local AI: route by successful-output value per joule, not just model size or confidence.
featured.result: Reported replay-mode cost-per-success down 32.06% and joules-per-success down 27.92%; live canary cost-per-success down 79.44% and joules-per-success down 82.52%, with rollback guards passing.
featured.bounds: Small models, single host, short-duration canary.

featured.title: Memory Pressure Admission Gate
featured.why_it_matters: A practical serving-control result that matches the Enoch worldview: protect the lane before requests create pathological pressure.
featured.result: Reported 69.0% lower p95 request latency and 71.0% lower energy proxy with zero completion loss on a compact live llama.cpp benchmark.
featured.bounds: Single stack, one quantized model, 16-request deterministic workload; peak RSS increased slightly.

featured.title: Cache Churn Alarm vLLM Adapter Benchmark
featured.why_it_matters: This is a nuanced artifact: it does not oversell latency, but it finds a concrete memory-pressure control signal for suppressing optional speculative branches.
featured.result: Under pressure conditions, reported optional speculative branches suppressed after one alarm transition, vLLM preemptions reduced from 2 to 0, joules per correct request improved 42.63%, and required-request quality preserved at 16/16.
featured.bounds: Single model/configuration and isolated A/B runs; p95 latency did not improve.
